topic: terminal WS auth outage on daax.galway
decision: Wire DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET into the /opt/daax deployment (.env + docker-compose.yml env entry with :? fail-closed) and recreate the daax container; no code change.
context: Image rebuilt 2026-07-03 introduced WS upgrade authentication (F1b #95). /opt/daax/docker-compose.yml was never updated to pass DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET, so POST /api/terminal/ticket returned 503, clients connected ticketless, and the terminal server rejected every upgrade from the Docker bridge gateway (1008 unauthorized) — all AI coding windows and shell terminals dead.
verification: Mint returns 200; WS upgrade with ticket accepted and spawns container session; upgrade without ticket still rejected 1008; container healthy; no leaked agent containers.
followup: Next image rebuild from current main includes default-deny middleware (#181) + posture gate (#184): forwarded identity will be REFUSED with HOST=0.0.0.0 unless DAAX_PROXY_SECRET is set in the app AND Traefik injects X-Daax-Proxy-Secret (see deploy/traefik-daax.yml.tpl inject-proxy-secret) with DAAX_REQUIRE_AUTH=1. Update /etc/traefik/dynamic/daax.yml + /opt/daax/.env BEFORE deploying a newer image, or the whole API plane 401s.

topic: stage proxy-secret trust chain for next image rebuild
decision: Wired the full F1a proxy-secret boundary on daax.galway ahead of the next image rebuild: /etc/traefik/dynamic/daax.yml now strips client X-Daax-Proxy-Secret and injects the real value on the daax router (inject-proxy-secret, per deploy/traefik-daax.yml.tpl); /opt/daax/.env + docker-compose.yml now set DAAX_PROXY_SECRET (fail-closed :?) and DAAX_REQUIRE_AUTH=1; daax container recreated.
ordering: Traefik injection applied BEFORE app-side secret so the running app (which ignores the unknown header until the secret is set) never broke mid-change.
verification: Traefik API confirms middleware enabled and in daax router chain; all galway routes respond unchanged. Live app matrix: headers+correct secret=200, spoofed header=401, wrong secret=401, no creds=401; WS ticketed upgrade accepted, bare upgrade 1008. Future-code proof: ran current main's evaluateAuthDecision + ws-auth authenticateConnection with the staged env — allow-user / deny / deny / ticket-ok / bare-rejected.
remaining: A rebuild from current main ALSO needs Postgres (DATABASE_URL + postgres + migrate services, repo deploy/docker-compose.yml is canonical) — /opt/daax compose has none; catalog/releases/RBAC surfaces depend on it. Do NOT rebuild until the compose stack is synced or data-less pages are acceptable.

decision: Added the canonical postgres + migrate services (mirrored from repo deploy/docker-compose.yml: digest-pinned postgres:18-alpine, parent-dir PGDATA volume mount, pg_isready gate, fail-closed one-shot migrate) and DATABASE_URL to /opt/daax/docker-compose.yml; daax now depends_on migrate service_completed_successfully.
obstacles: (1) Pre-existing daax-pg-data volume (2026-07-01, schema-only, all tables empty — no export needed; POSTGRES_PASSWORD ignored on existing data) → aligned role password via ALTER USER. (2) ~/.zshrc lines 171-172 exported STALE DAAX_PG_PASSWORD/DAAX_WS_TOKEN_SECRET after sourcing ~/.secrets; docker compose prefers process env over .env, so composes kept getting the stale password → commented out in zshrc (backup ~/.zshrc.backup.20260706_daax_secrets); this session's shell snapshot still carries them, so compose was run with env -u overrides. (3) Quoted values copied from ~/.secrets into /opt/daax/.env normalized (compose strips quotes; comparisons/URLs must too).
verification: migrate exits 0 (schema up to date, 3 migrations); daax healthy; DATABASE_URL/WS secret in container match .env; GET /api/releases + /api/catalog/bases 200 via authed matrix; WRITE ROUND-TRIP: POST /api/releases -> row visible in PG via psql -> DELETE -> row gone; app holds live PG connection; terminal WS e2e still green (mint 200, ticketed session spawned); no leaked agent containers; logs clean.
note: Legacy SQLite catalog.db/releases.db do not exist in the data volume — nothing to export.
